name: WFBE_NEURODEF_AAPOS_HEAVY_WEST
notes: AA BATTERY / SAM SITE (HEAVY, 4 AI) — WEST. This is the premium air-defence site, so it is laid out like a real battery, not a cluster. Soldier logic: THREE Stinger launchers spread on a wide 18 m arc (+Y) so counter-battery / a strafing run cannot gut the battery in one pass — each in its own sandbag pit. Earthen ramparts (revetments) sit BETWEEN and beside the launchers to eat shrapnel from near-misses without ever capping the upward shot. A camo-netted control/radar point sits centre-rear (the 'brains' — dispersed back from the guns it cues). Local security is a rear .50 covering the whole blind side. Two ammo points are split left/right so one lucky hit does not disarm the battery. HESCO wings screen the flanks; wire closes the forward approach; the rear stays open as the crew's covered entrance/exit.

objs.role: SAM launcher 1 (far left) — arc NW, wide dispersion
SAM launcher 3 (far right) — arc NE, wide dispersion
revetment berm — shrapnel screen between L1 and L2
revetment berm — shrapnel screen between L2 and L3
control/radar point — battery command, dispersed rear
camo net over the control point
local security .50 — covers rear/left blind side
